qas.q:
You spot this potential HazMat scene and try to identify the materials present.

Identify THREE additional steps you need to take.

- Notify the IC.
- Control the scene and keep everyone out.
- Attack the fire.
- Approach the scene to identify other possible HazMats.
- Warn others in the immediate vicinity.
- Call animal control.
qas.a:
- Notify the IC.
- Control the scene and keep everyone out.
- Warn others in the immediate vicinity.

qas.q:
Match each section of the ERG with the appropriate content description.

- Hazardous material names listed alphabetically.
- Placards and colors.
- Identification numbers (UN/NA) listed consecutively.
- Evacuation distances.
- Hazards of an identified material.
qas.a:
- Hazardous material names listed alphabetically. (BLUE SECTION)

- Placards and colors. (WHITE PAGES)

- Identification numbers (UN/NA) listed consecutively. (YELLOW SECTION)

- Evacuation distances. (GREEN SECTION)

- Hazards of an identified material. (ORANGE SECTION)

qas.q:
Identify THREE terms that are NOT part of the DECIDE process.

- Explain
- Choose
- Detect
- Do
- Examine
- Investigate

The acronym describing the series of proper decisions to make for reducing a hazardous materials incident's potential harm is:

- HAZMAT
- GEBMO
- DECIDE
- OCMPSS

Identify the most dangerous hazardous material detection clue to rely on.

- Container shapes.
- Occupancy and location.
- Your senses.
- Placards and labels.

Identify the THREE main hazards of hazardous materials.

- Explosive
- Runoff
- Fire
- Legal
- Health

Identify THREE steps for wildland firefighters to take at a hazardous materials incident.

- Isolate the area to ensure the safety of people and the environment.
- Move closer to determine what you are dealing with.
- Quickly put out the fire.
- Allow room for equipment movement.
- Move people from the scene to a safe area.
qas.a:
- Isolate the area to ensure the safety of people and the environment.
- Allow room for equipment movement.
- Move people from the scene to a safe area.

qas.q:
Hazardous materials can be a _____ material, or a combination of _____ materials.

- crated, burning
- normal, dangerous
- dry, liquid
- single, several

Identify the guidebook that is an excellent resource for more information about hazardous materials.

- Chemical Field Guide (CFG)
- Emergency Response Guidebook (ERG)
- Incident Response Hazmat Guide (IRHG)
- Wildland Hazards Resource Guide (WHRG)

There are six main detection clues to look for at a HazMat incident.

Match each clue with an example of what to look for.

- Cylindrical tanks or rounded ends
- Names, numbering, or stenciling
- Diamond-shaped images
- Type of materials manufactured, stored, or used
- Documents listing chemical name, number, and type
- Vapor clouds and hissing noises
qas.a:
- Cylindrical tanks or rounded ends (CONTAINER SHAPES)
- Names, numbering, or stenciling (MARKINGS AND COLORS)
- Diamond-shaped images (PLACARDS AND LABELS)
- Type of materials manufactured, stored, or used (OCCUPANCY AND LOCATION)
- Documents listing chemical name, number, and type (SHIPPING PAPERS)
- Vapor clouds and hissing noises (SENSES)

qas.q:
Identify THREE hazards you might commonly encounter at farms and ranches.

- Clandestine drug labs
- Dynamite
- Booby traps
- Bulk chemicals
- Aboveground diesel tanks
qas.a:
- Dynamite
- Bulk chemicals
- Aboveground diesel tanks

qas.q:
While fighting fire in a wildland environment, you see in the distance a fenced in area with large cylindrical tanks. You are already uphill and upwind of the area.

The FIRST step you should take is to:

- Continue with the firefighting.
- Attempt to identify the material by using binoculars.
- Run away from the area.
- Notify the incident commander (IC) that there is a potential problem.
qas.a: - Attempt to identify the material by using binoculars.

qas.q:
Match each element of the DECIDE process with the appropriate description.

- The best option
- Progress
- The likely harm and size of the material
-Action options
- A response objective
- Any hazardous materials present
qas.a:
- The best option (DO)

- Progress (EVALUATE)

- The likely harm and size of the material (ESTIMATE)

-Action options (IDENTIFY)

- A response objective (CHOOSE)

- Any hazardous materials present (DETECT)
